words.definitionEn: to leave somebody, especially somebody you are responsible for, with no intention of returning
words.exampleEn: abandon somebody, The baby had been abandoned by its mother.

words.definitionEn: the act or process of finishing something; the state of being finished and complete
words.exampleEn: the completion of the new hospital building

words.definitionEn: a group of buildings of a similar type together in one place
words.exampleEn: a sports/leisure/shopping complex

words.definitionEn: made of many different things or parts that are connected; difficult to understand
words.exampleEn: a complicated issue/process/system

words.definitionEn: one of several parts of which something is made
words.exampleEn: the different organizations involved in the design of the various components

words.definitionEn: to take somebody else’s child into your family and become its legal parent(s)
words.exampleEn: a campaign to encourage childless couples to adopt

words.definitionEn: to write music
words.exampleEn: Mozart composed his last opera shortly before he died.

words.definitionEn: a person who writes music, especially classical music
words.exampleEn: Verdi was a prolific composer of operas.

words.definitionEn: a thing consisting of two or more separate things combined together
words.exampleEn: compounds derived from rainforest plants

words.definitionEn: including all, or almost all, the items, details, facts, information, etc., that may be involved
words.exampleEn: a comprehensive list of addresses

words.definitionEn: to have somebody/something as parts or members
words.exampleEn: comprise something, The collection comprises 327 paintings.

words.definitionEn: that must be done because of a law or a rule
words.exampleEn: English is a compulsory subject at this level.

words.definitionEn: the ability to direct all your effort and attention on one thing, without thinking of other things
words.exampleEn: This book requires a great deal of concentration.

words.definitionEn: an idea or a principle that is connected with something abstract
words.exampleEn: concept of something, the concept of social class

words.definitionEn: a feeling of worry, especially one that is shared by many people
words.exampleEn: concern about something/somebody, Villagers expressed concern about the level of traffic.

words.definitionEn: to affect somebody/something; to involve somebody/something
words.exampleEn: Don't interfere in what doesn't concern you.

words.definitionEn: worried and feeling concern about something/somebody
words.exampleEn: Concerned citizens can have a huge impact.

words.definitionEn: made of concrete
words.exampleEn: a concrete floor

words.definitionEn: building material that is made by mixing together cement, sand, small stones and water
words.exampleEn: a slab of concrete

words.definitionEn: a person’s behaviour in a particular place or in a particular situation
words.exampleEn: The sport has a strict code of conduct.

words.definitionEn: to organize and/or do a particular activity
words.exampleEn: to conduct an experiment/inquiry/investigation/interview

words.definitionEn: to admit, especially formally or to the police, that you have done something wrong or illegal
words.exampleEn: After hours of questioning, the suspect confessed.

words.definitionEn: the feeling that you can trust, believe in and be sure about the abilities or good qualities of somebody/something
words.exampleEn: The president's actions hardly inspire confidence.

words.definitionEn: done or given before something is going to happen
words.exampleEn: Please give us advance warning of any changes.

words.definitionEn: a situation in which people, groups or countries disagree strongly or are involved in a serious argument
words.exampleEn: The violence was the result of political conflicts.

words.definitionEn: if two ideas, beliefs, stories, etc. conflict, it is not possible for them to exist together or for them both to be true
words.exampleEn: Reports conflicted on how much of the aid was reaching the famine victims.

words.definitionEn: difficult to understand; not clear
words.exampleEn: The instructions on the box are very confusing.

words.definitionEn: a state of not being certain about what is happening, what you should do, what something means, etc.
words.exampleEn: The announcement caused a lot of confusion.

words.definitionEn: progress or a development in a particular activity or area of understanding
words.exampleEn: We live in an age of rapid technological advance.

words.definitionEn: aware of something; noticing something
words.exampleEn: conscious of something, She's very conscious of the problems involved.

words.definitionEn: if knowledge, technology, etc. advances, it develops and improves
words.exampleEn: Technology is advancing at an incredibly rapid pace.

words.definitionEn: as a result; therefore
words.exampleEn: This poses a threat to agriculture and the food chain, and consequently to human health.

words.definitionEn: the protection of the natural environment
words.exampleEn: to be interested in wildlife conservation

words.definitionEn: opposed to great or sudden social change; showing that you prefer traditional styles and values
words.exampleEn: the conservative views of his parents

words.definitionEn: a conservative person
words.exampleEn: social/religious conservatives

words.definitionEn: great in amount, size, importance, etc.
words.exampleEn: The project wasted a considerable amount of time and money.

words.definitionEn: much; a lot
words.exampleEn: The need for sleep varies considerably from person to person.

words.definitionEn: the act of thinking carefully about something
words.exampleEn: An employer is legally bound to give due consideration to the request.

words.definitionEn: always behaving in the same way, or having the same opinions, standards, etc.
words.exampleEn: a consistent approach to the problem

words.definitionEn: always the same
words.exampleEn: Her work has been of a consistently high standard.

words.definitionEn: a secret plan by a group of people to do something harmful or illegal
words.exampleEn: conspiracy (to do something), a conspiracy to overthrow the government

words.definitionEn: happening all the time or repeatedly
words.exampleEn: There were constant interruptions.

words.definitionEn: all the time; repeatedly
words.exampleEn: Fashion is constantly changing.

words.definitionEn: to build or make something such as a road, building or machine
words.exampleEn: construct something, The building was constructed in 1993.

words.definitionEn: the process or method of building or making something, especially roads, buildings, bridges, etc.
words.exampleEn: Construction began this year and will take approximately 18 months.

words.definitionEn: to go to somebody for information or advice
words.exampleEn: consult somebody, If the pain continues, consult your doctor.

words.definitionEn: a person who knows a lot about a particular subject and is employed to give advice about it to other people
words.exampleEn: a technology/design consultant

words.definitionEn: the act of using energy, food or materials; the amount used
words.exampleEn: the production of fuel for domestic consumption (= to be used in the country where it is produced)

words.definitionEn: belonging to the same time
words.exampleEn: We have no contemporary account of the battle (= written near the time that it happened).

words.definitionEn: a competition in which people try to win something
words.exampleEn: to hold a singing/talent contest

words.definitionEn: to take part in a competition, election, etc. and try to win it
words.exampleEn: Three candidates contested the leadership.

words.definitionEn: an official written agreement
words.exampleEn: All employees have a written contract of employment.

words.definitionEn: to become less or smaller; to make something become less or smaller
words.exampleEn: Glass contracts as it cools.

words.definitionEn: to give something, especially money or goods, to help achieve or provide something
words.exampleEn: Do you wish to contribute?

words.definitionEn: a gift or payment that is made to a person or an organization in order to help pay for something
words.exampleEn: All contributions will be gratefully received.

words.definitionEn: causing a lot of angry public discussion and disagreement
words.exampleEn: a highly controversial topic

words.definitionEn: public discussion and argument about something that many people strongly disagree about, think is bad, or are shocked by
words.exampleEn: to arouse/cause controversy

words.definitionEn: the quality of being useful, easy or suitable for somebody
words.exampleEn: We have provided seats for the convenience of our customers.

words.definitionEn: the way in which something is done that most people in a society expect and consider to be polite or the right way to do it
words.exampleEn: the rigid social conventions of Victorian Britain

words.definitionEn: tending to follow what is done or considered acceptable by society in general; normal and ordinary, and perhaps not very interesting
words.exampleEn: conventional behaviour/morality

words.definitionEn: to change or make something change from one form, purpose, system, etc. to another
words.exampleEn: convert something, They took just nine months to convert the building.

words.definitionEn: to make ideas, feelings, etc. known to somebody
words.exampleEn: convey something, Colours like red convey a sense of energy and strength.

words.definitionEn: completely sure about something
words.exampleEn: Sam nodded but he didn't look convinced.

words.definitionEn: that makes somebody believe that something is true
words.exampleEn: a convincing argument/explanation/case

words.definitionEn: to deal successfully with something difficult
words.exampleEn: I got to the stage where I wasn't coping any more.

words.definitionEn: most important; main or essential
words.exampleEn: core subjects (= subjects that all the students have to study) such as English and mathematics

words.definitionEn: the most important or central part of something
words.exampleEn: This report goes to the core of the argument.

words.definitionEn: connected with a large business company
words.exampleEn: corporate finance/profits/tax

words.definitionEn: a large business company
words.exampleEn: multinational corporations

words.definitionEn: a long narrow passage in a building, with doors that open into rooms on either side
words.exampleEn: His room is along the corridor.

words.definitionEn: a group of people who are elected to govern an area such as a city or county
words.exampleEn: a town council

words.definitionEn: a long flat surface over which goods are sold or business is done in a shop, bank, etc.
words.exampleEn: behind the counter, I asked the woman behind the counter if they had any postcards.

words.definitionEn: events that are of public interest or political importance
words.exampleEn: world/international affairs

words.definitionEn: one of a number of areas that some countries are divided into, each with its own local government
words.exampleEn: rural counties west of the Mississippi

words.definitionEn: the ability to do something dangerous, or to face pain or opposition, without showing fear
words.exampleEn: He showed great courage and determination.

words.definitionEn: the reporting of news and sport in the media
words.exampleEn: media/newspaper/press coverage

words.definitionEn: a line on the surface of something where it has broken but not split into separate parts
words.exampleEn: This cup has a crack in it.

words.definitionEn: to break without dividing into separate parts; to break something in this way
words.exampleEn: The ice cracked as I stepped onto it.

words.definitionEn: an activity involving a special skill at making things with your hands
words.exampleEn: traditional crafts like basket-weaving

words.definitionEn: an accident in which a vehicle hits something, for example another vehicle, usually causing damage and often injuring or killing the passengers
words.exampleEn: a car/plane crash

words.definitionEn: if a vehicle crashes or the driver crashes it, it hits an object or another vehicle, causing damage
words.exampleEn: I was terrified that the plane would crash.

words.definitionEn: the act or process of making something that is new, or of causing something to exist that did not exist before
words.exampleEn: Job creation needs to be the top priority.

words.definitionEn: the use of skill and imagination to produce something new or to produce art
words.exampleEn: Creativity and originality are more important than technical skill.

words.definitionEn: a living thing, real or imaginary, that can move around, such as an animal
words.exampleEn: respect for all living creatures

words.definitionEn: to add an amount of money to somebody’s bank account
words.exampleEn: credit something, They credited my account two days later.

words.definitionEn: all the people working on a ship, plane, etc.
words.exampleEn: crew members

words.definitionEn: cheap enough that people can afford to pay it or buy it
words.exampleEn: We offer quality products at affordable prices.

words.definitionEn: a time of great danger, difficulty or doubt when problems must be solved or important decisions must be made
words.exampleEn: an economic/a financial crisis

words.definitionEn: a standard or principle by which something is judged, or with the help of which a decision is made
words.exampleEn: The main criterion is value for money.

words.definitionEn: a person who expresses opinions about the good and bad qualities of books, music, etc.
words.exampleEn: a film/an art/a music critic

words.definitionEn: saying what you think is bad about somebody/something
words.exampleEn: You should just ignore any critical comments.

words.definitionEn: in a way that says what you think is bad about somebody/something
words.exampleEn: She spoke critically of her father.

words.definitionEn: the act of expressing disapproval of somebody/something and opinions about their faults or bad qualities; a statement showing this disapproval
words.exampleEn: to draw/face/receive criticism

words.definitionEn: to say that you think somebody/something is bad; to say what you do not like or think is wrong about somebody/something
words.exampleEn: All you ever do is criticize!

words.definitionEn: a plant that is grown in large quantities, especially as food
words.exampleEn: Sugar is an important crop on the island.

words.definitionEn: extremely important, because it will affect other things
words.exampleEn: The next few weeks are going to be crucial.

words.definitionEn: a journey by sea, visiting different places, especially as a holiday
words.exampleEn: I'd love to go on a round-the-world cruise.

words.definitionEn: to travel in a ship or boat visiting different places, especially as a holiday
words.exampleEn: (+ adv./prep.), They cruised down the Nile.

words.definitionEn: a loud sound without words that expresses a strong feeling
words.exampleEn: cry of something, to give a cry of anguish/despair/pain/joy/alarm, etc.

words.definitionEn: an action or event that is a signal for somebody to do something
words.exampleEn: cue (for something), Jon's arrival was a cue for more champagne.

words.definitionEn: a medicine or medical treatment that cures an illness
words.exampleEn: cure for something, the search for a cure for cancer

words.definitionEn: to make a person or an animal healthy again after an illness
words.exampleEn: cure somebody, Will you be able to cure him, Doctor?

words.definitionEn: having a strong desire to know about something
words.exampleEn: He is such a curious boy, always asking questions.

words.definitionEn: the movement of water in the sea or a river; the movement of air in a particular direction
words.exampleEn: with the current, It's easier to go with the current.

words.definitionEn: the subjects that are included in a course of study or taught in a school, college, etc.
words.exampleEn: The school curriculum should be as broad as possible.

words.definitionEn: a line or surface that bends gradually; a smooth bend
words.exampleEn: curve of something, He admired the delicate curve of her ear.

words.definitionEn: to move or make something move in the shape of a curve; to be in the shape of a curve
words.exampleEn: a curving staircase

words.definitionEn: having a round shape
words.exampleEn: a curved edge/surface

words.definitionEn: total and complete
words.exampleEn: I've joined a class for absolute beginners.

words.definitionEn: pretty and attractive
words.exampleEn: a cute little baby

words.definitionEn: made from milk
words.exampleEn: dairy products/produce

words.definitionEn: milk, cheese and other milk products
words.exampleEn: The doctor told me to eat less red meat and dairy.

words.definitionEn: at a later time; after an event that has already been mentioned
words.exampleEn: Afterwards she was sorry for what she'd said.

words.definitionEn: to be brave enough to do something
words.exampleEn: She said it as loudly as she dared.

words.definitionEn: the state of being dark, without any light
words.exampleEn: After a few minutes our eyes got used to the darkness.

words.definitionEn: an organized set of data that is stored in a computer and can be looked at and used in various ways
words.exampleEn: The database is updated monthly.

words.definitionEn: to write or print the date on something
words.exampleEn: Thank you for your letter dated 24th March.

words.definitionEn: a point in time by which something must be done
words.exampleEn: I prefer to work to a deadline.

words.definitionEn: causing or likely to cause death
words.exampleEn: a deadly weapon/disease

words.definitionEn: a person whose business is buying and selling a particular product
words.exampleEn: an art/antique dealer

words.definitionEn: a formal discussion of an issue at a public meeting or in a parliament. In a debate two or more speakers express opposite views and then there is often a vote on the issue.
words.exampleEn: the first ever televised presidential debate

words.definitionEn: to discuss something, especially formally, before making a decision or finding a solution
words.exampleEn: debate (something), Politicians will be debating the bill later this week.

words.definitionEn: a sum of money that somebody owes
words.exampleEn: to pay/repay a debt

words.definitionEn: of a good enough standard or quality
words.exampleEn: a decent meal/place to live

words.definitionEn: the top outside floor of a ship or boat
words.exampleEn: on deck, I was the only person on deck at that time of night.

words.definitionEn: to say something officially or publicly
words.exampleEn: declare something, The government has declared a state of emergency.

words.definitionEn: a continuous decrease in the number, value, quality, etc. of something
words.exampleEn: decline in something, There has been a 5 per cent decline in student numbers.

words.definitionEn: to become smaller, fewer, weaker, etc.
words.exampleEn: Support for the party continues to decline.

words.definitionEn: a thing that makes something look more attractive on special occasions
words.exampleEn: Christmas/festive decorations

words.definitionEn: the process of reducing something; the amount that something is reduced by
words.exampleEn: Sales for May show a decrease compared with the same month last year.

words.definitionEn: to become smaller in size, number, etc.; to make something smaller in size, number, etc.
words.exampleEn: Donations have decreased significantly over the past few years.

words.definitionEn: very; very much
words.exampleEn: She is deeply religious.

words.definitionEn: failure to win or to be successful
words.exampleEn: The party faces defeat in the election.

words.definitionEn: to win against somebody in a war, competition, sports game, etc.
words.exampleEn: defeat somebody/something, He defeated the champion in three sets.

words.definitionEn: the act of protecting somebody/something from attack, criticism, etc.
words.exampleEn: in defence of somebody/something, soldiers who died in defence of their country
